description_long: This object is a cylindrical, stick-handled bomb resembling the classic German Model 24 Stielhandgranate, commonly known as a 'stick grenade.' The main body consists of a dark metal cylindrical head attached to a long, wooden handle, which is slightly tapered and shows a natural wood grain texture. A prominent metal clip or lever is affixed to the side of the cylindrical head, likely serving as a safety or triggering mechanism. The metal head appears robust and industrial, with a matte, slightly worn finish, while the handle is smooth and ergonomically shaped for gripping. The overall construction suggests a combination of metal and wood, with the head likely containing internal explosive material. No visible warning labels, wires, or timer displays are present on the exterior.
description: A stick-handled cylindrical bomb with a metal head and wooden handle, featuring a side-mounted metal lever. The design is robust and utilitarian, with a matte finish and no visible labels or electronics.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bomb, highlighting the cylindrical metal head, wooden handle, and a prominent metal lever on the left side.
description_view_1: End-on view from the handle, showing the circular cross-section of the handle and the alignment of the head and lever.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the cylindrical head and handle, with the metal lever now on the right side.
description_view_3: End-on view from the head, showing the circular face of the metal head and the attachment point of the lever.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The bomb appears to use a mechanical lever or pull-activation system, likely requiring the user to pull or twist the lever to initiate detonation.
